data.title: Bitcoin spot ETF had a total net inflow of $86.3114 million yesterday, continuing its net inflow for 4 consecutive days
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (June 12, Eastern Time), the total net inflow of Bitcoin spot ETFs was 86.3114 million US dollars.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net inflow in a single day yesterday was Blackrock ETF IBIT, with a net inflow of 288 million US dollars in a single day. Currently, IBIT's total net inflow in history has reached 49.527 billion US dollars.
The second is Grayscale ETF GBTC, with a net inflow of 5.8919 million US dollars in a single day, and the total net outflow of GBTC in history has reached 23.248 billion US dollars.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net outflow in a single day yesterday was Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a net outflow of 197 million US dollars in a single day, and the total net inflow of FBTC in history has reached 11.493 billion US dollars.
As of press time, the total net asset value of the Bitcoin spot ETF is US$130.263 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market value to the total market value of Bitcoin) is 6.18%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$45.309 billion.

data.title: A whale once again transferred $6.55 million worth of PEPE to Binance, with a total floating loss of $3.26 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale further transferred $6.55 million worth of PEPE to Binance. Overall, the whale deposited 1.6T PEPE worth $18.1 million to Binance and faced a loss of $3.26 million. The whale still has 600 billion PEPE in its wallet, worth $6.39 million.

data.title: SmartMall released a new series of NFTs today and simultaneously launched the "Group Buying" model
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official reports, SmartMall, a Web3 smart application aggregation traffic platform, released a new series of NFTs today and simultaneously launched a "group purchase" model. Users can form teams to purchase collectively at a lower price. After the group purchase is successful, team members can also unlock additional rewards.

data.title: Trump's birthday military parade will be held on Saturday, and "no king" protests will break out simultaneously in 2,000 cities across the United States
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News This Saturday (June 14) is a major day that coincides with the 250th anniversary of the founding of the US Army, Trump's 79th birthday and the US Flag Day. Trump said earlier that he would "make a big deal" on this day and hold a large-scale military parade. The event specifications will "surpass the World Cup and the Olympics". Cannons, soldiers and fireworks will be the highlights of the event, aiming to create a "spectacle". 
At the same time, Trump's opponents also hope to stage a larger-scale protest across the United States on that day. 
Progressive groups including the American Civil Liberties Union, the American Federation of Teachers, Planned Parenthood and Senator Bernie Sanders' campaign office are planning to hold peaceful protests in more than 2,000 places across the United States. 
These demonstrations are called "No Kings Day" and are aimed at opposing what organizers call Trump's overstepping of power. Event leaders expect turnout to exceed the Hands Off protest in April, which drew at least 3.5 million people. It will likely be the largest anti-demonstration during Trump's second term, comparable to the Women's March during his first.

Trump has warned this week that protesters will be met with "very strong force" at his parade. The Pentagon says the cost of a presidential parade could be as high as $45 million. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Japan's Inflation Trends May Prompt Interest Rate Discussions
data.description: According to Odaily, insiders have revealed that officials at the Bank of Japan believe the recent rise in prices has slightly exceeded earlier expectations this year. This development could open the door for discussions on raising interest rates if global trade tensions ease. Insiders indicate that the Bank of Japan is expected to maintain its benchmark interest rate at 0.5% following the conclusion of a two-day meeting next week. This decision is based on the need to monitor the progress of global tariff negotiations and their impact on the economy. However, if tariff measures prove to be less disruptive to core price trends, officials may consider this as support for discussing a potential rate hike. These statements suggest that if the global trade situation becomes clearer, the Bank of Japan might consider raising interest rates by the end of the year.

data.title: AMD Unveils Advanced AI Chips at Global Development Conference
data.description:
According to PANews, AMD hosted the 2025 Global AI Development Conference early today, unveiling its latest AI chips and cloud infrastructure hardware. OpenAI co-founder and CEO Sam Altman attended as a special guest, collaborating with AMD to introduce the Instinct MI400 and Instinct MI350 series of powerful AI chips. Throughout the development process, OpenAI provided technical feedback to AMD, aiding in GPU optimization.

During the event, Sam Altman expressed astonishment upon learning that a single MI400 chip is equipped with 432GB of HBM4 memory, exclaiming disbelief at the specifications. Compared to previous models, the MI350 features 288GB of HBM3E memory and boasts a memory bandwidth of up to 8TB/s, enhancing AI computing capabilities by four times and inference performance by 35 times.

AMD highlighted that due to lower power consumption compared to competitors, the MI355X offers 40% more tokens per dollar than NVIDIA chips.

data.title: Bank of Japan expects stronger-than-expected inflation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to people familiar with the matter, Bank of Japan officials believe that price increases are slightly higher than expected earlier this year, a factor that may open the door to discussing whether to raise interest rates as global trade tensions ease. People familiar with the matter said that officials expect the Bank of Japan's benchmark interest rate to remain unchanged at 0.5% at the end of the two-day meeting next week because they need to monitor the progress of global tariff negotiations and their impact on the economy. But people familiar with the matter said that if the tariff measures prove to be not too destructive to the underlying price trends, officials will consider this to support the discussion of whether to raise interest rates. The remarks of these people indicate that if the global trade situation becomes clearer, the Bank of Japan may raise interest rates before the end of this year. (Jinshi)

data.title: HYPE's second largest individual holder pledged 3.35 million tokens 10 hours ago, worth more than $130 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens monitoring, 10 hours ago, HYPE's second largest individual holder @laurentzeimes had pledged 3.35 million HYPE, worth more than US$130.69 million.

data.title: Trump: Central Command on high alert
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: US President Trump said in an interview with Fox News that Iran could not have nuclear weapons and he still hoped that Iran could return to the negotiation table. Currently, he is closely watching possible retaliation by Iran and the US Central Command is on high alert. In addition, Trump said that he had been informed of the situation before Israel launched the attack. Earlier, US officials said that the United States still hopes to hold talks with Iran on Sunday. (Jinshi)

data.title: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu Announces Successful Strike
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that the country has successfully launched its first strike. This development marks a significant moment in Israel's defense strategy. Further details about the operation were not disclosed.

data.title: CFTC Acting Chair Reaffirms Commitment to Cryptocurrency Regulation
data.description: According to Foresight News, Cointelegraph reports that Caroline Pham, the Acting Chair of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), emphasized that the agency will not ease cryptocurrency regulations despite the Trump administration's promises to support the crypto industry. Pham stated that while the CFTC supports innovation and growth, this does not grant immunity from regulatory oversight. She clarified that the focus is not on criminalizing asset classes or technologies but on addressing issues such as lies, deception, and theft.
